Off Calendar Jan-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Jenny Jin, (415) 835-5900 of the Hooshmand Law Group for Plaintiffs/Opposing party.
Yvette Davis, (510)465-3922 of Ranlin, Sproat, Mires, et al for Defendants/Moving party Dahlia Hotel, LLC, SanJay Patel and Vijay Patel.
James Burns, (510)254-6768 of Kronenberg Law for Defendant/Moving Dhiren Guadriya.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DAHLIA HOTEL, LLC, SANJAY PATEL, VIJAY PATEL AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The applicable statute of limitations as to the cause of action for SFRO violation is 1 year; the applicable statute of limitations as to all other causes of action is 2 years. To the extent that some of the complained about acts occurred outside the applicable statute of limitations and some within the statute of limitations as to each particular Plaintiff, the demurrer as to the whole cause of action/and complaint is overruled.
(1) The demurrer to the cause of action for SFRO violation is sustained without leave to amend as to all Plaintiffs who vacated the premises 1 year before the filing of the complaint.
(2) The demurer as to all other causes of action is sustained without leave to amend as to all Plaintiffs who vacated the premises 2 years before the filing of the complaint.
(3) The demurrer as to the cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress cause of action is overruled. All elements of this cause of action are sufficiently alleged.
(4) The demurer to the cause of action for injunctive relief is sustained without leave to amend as to all Plaintiffs who do not currently reside at the premises. No substantive opposition filed.
Defendant is ordered to prepare a proposed order outlining the ruling as to each Plaintiff in accordance with this Court's general ruling as specified above. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
